and folk artist Bob Short,<|9.34|> <|9.34|> a depiction of the devil in a business suit with the seven deadly sins listed.<|17.50|> <|17.50|> And the painting is entitled, "Man Created Satan in His Own Image."<|25.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I love this passage for its ability to tell the whole of the gospel message in 15 short lines.<|12.00|> <|12.00|> As described by Mita Stamper, a Markan scholar, and she uses a fabulous Latin phrase, "in nuque," in a nutshell.<|23.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> briefly stated. She writes, "At the center is Jesus' victory for the kingdom of God, the<|10.88|> <|10.88|> subversion of the strong man by the stronger one, and the freeing of the plunder, God's<|18.76|> <|18.76|> good creation."<|20.60|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> We also see in this passage how we are claimed by Jesus as his siblings and parents,<|7.98|> <|7.98|> no longer on the outside or at a distance, but holders of the secrets of the kingdom,<|16.66|> <|17.60|> drawn into the inner circle of the mystery and love of God.<|23.24|> <|23.24|> Amen.<|23.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> This, my friends, is the message of the gospel.<|5.30|> <|6.64|> And it is a message that still sends those who hold tightly to power and desire to oppress.<|15.96|> <|16.96|> It throws them still into a panic.<|19.24|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|2.42|><|endoftext|>
